subject: Science
chapter: Changes Around Us: Physical and Chemical

activities_projects.title: Crystallization of Alum
activities_projects.objective: Obtain pure crystals through a physical process
steps: Prepare a saturated solution of alum in hot water
Filter the solution into a clean glass
Suspend a small 'seed' crystal using a thread
Leave it overnight and observe the growth of large, well-defined crystals
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands crystallization as a method of purification and a physical change
activities_projects.evaluation: Quality of crystals formed and explanation of saturation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The Magnesium Ribbon Mystery
activities_projects.objective: Study the chemical change of burning a metal
steps: Clean the magnesium ribbon with sandpaper
Hold it with tongs and burn it; collect the white ash
Dissolve the ash in a little water
Test the solution with litmus paper to see if a new substance was formed
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Identifies the formation of a new substance (Magnesium Oxide) as a chemical change
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct identification of the basic nature of the ash solution (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Vinegar and Baking Soda Volcano
activities_projects.objective: Observe gas evolution as a sign of chemical change
steps: Place baking soda in a jar and add vinegar
Observe the rapid fizzing and bubble formation
Lower a burning matchstick into the mouth of the jar
Observe the flame go out due to Carbon Dioxide
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Recognizes that the production of a new gas is a characteristic of chemical change
activities_projects.evaluation: Explanation of the chemical reaction and gas identification (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Copper Sulphate and Iron Reaction
steps: Make a blue solution of copper sulphate
Drop a clean iron nail into the solution
Wait for 30 minutes and observe the color shift from blue to green
Check the nail for a brown copper coating
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands that a change in color and the formation of a deposit indicate a chemical change
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy in describing the displacement process (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The Curdling of Milk
activities_projects.objective: Analyze the chemical transformation of milk into curd
materials: A spoonful of curd or lemon juice
steps: Add a little curd to warm milk and keep it in a warm place
Wait for 5-6 hours until the milk sets
Compare the taste, texture, and smell of the milk vs. the curd
Try to reverse the curd back to milk
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Explains why curdling is a chemical change and is irreversible
activities_projects.evaluation: Comparison table of milk and curd properties (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Galvanization Observation
activities_projects.objective: Study methods to prevent chemical changes (rusting)
steps: Place both nails in a concentrated salt solution
Leave them for 48 hours
Observe which nail shows signs of corrosion
Explain how the zinc coating protects the iron
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands that preventing exposure to oxygen/moisture stops chemical changes
activities_projects.evaluation: Logical explanation of the protective layer (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Expansion on Heating
steps: Stretch a balloon over the mouth of an empty bottle
Place the bottle in hot water; observe the balloon inflate
Move the bottle to cold water; observe the balloon deflate
Discuss if the air inside changed into something new
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Demonstrates that expansion and contraction are physical changes
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct reasoning for why this is a reversible physical change (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Burning of Candle: Dual Change
activities_projects.objective: Identify both physical and chemical changes occurring together
steps: Light a candle and observe the melting wax (collect it as it cools)
Observe the burning wick and the release of smoke/heat
Identify which part is physical (melting) and which is chemical (burning)
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Recognizes that some processes involve both types of changes simultaneously
activities_projects.evaluation: Clearly distinguishing between the melting wax and burning wick (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Sublimation of Camphor
activities_projects.objective: Observe a direct change of state
steps: Place camphor in the dish and cover it with an inverted funnel
Plug the funnel stem with cotton and heat gently
Observe the camphor turn into gas and then deposit back on the funnel walls
Explain why this is physical
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands sublimation as a physical change where identity is retained
activities_projects.evaluation: Diagram and explanation of the sublimation process (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Change Lab Report
steps: Perform three minor changes (e.g., breaking a pencil, mixing salt in water, adding lemon to tea)
Record 'Initial State', 'Action', 'Resulting State'
Conclude if each was Physical or Chemical based on properties
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Strengthens scientific reporting and evidence-based conclusion
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy of the lab report and classification (10 marks)
